steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls inside a sentence that has two parts: a noun phrase ('the Hanseatic League') that renames 'the alliance,' and then a second independent clause beginning 'a loose confederation...' The punctuation in the blank must (1) set off that renaming phrase and (2) separate two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — centuries: the Hanseatic League,: A colon can introduce an appositive, but a comma cannot separate the two independent clauses that follow. ✗
  • Choice B — centuries—the Hanseatic League;: An em dash sets off the appositive 'the Hanseatic League,' and a semicolon correctly separates the two independent clauses on either side. ✓
  • Choice C — centuries; the Hanseatic League,: A semicolon here splits the first independent clause from its own appositive, breaking the sentence structure before the appositive is resolved. ✗
  • Choice D — centuries, the Hanseatic League,: Two commas cannot set off an appositive and also separate independent clauses; the result is a run-on. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
